candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A/impairment summary line above EBIT, but it is not labelled as COGS/cost of sales and appears to be a company-wide total.
Operating depreciation component of the summary line, but not identified as relating specifically to Materials and services / COGS.
Operating amortization component, but not identified as COGS-related.
Potential D&A component row, but isolated to one year and not labelled as COGS-related.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is 'Materials and services'. The visible D&A rows are separate operating lines at the same level as other operating expenses, not subcomponents of Materials and services, but none is explicitly tied to COGS/cost of sales. The main summary row also includes impairment, which is out of scope for D&A extraction. To avoid unsupported allocation and double counting, no COGS-specific D&A row is selected from this excerpt.

candidates.why: Separate operating-level D&A/impairment summary line above EBIT and outside the selected XSGA rows, but it is not labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead-specific.
Depreciation component of the operating D&A line, but not identified as SG&A-related.
Amortization component of the operating D&A line, but not identified as SG&A- or R&D-related.
Possible D&A component row in one year only, but not labelled as SG&A-specific.

variables.notes: Selected XSGA bucket is 'Selling/General/Administrative Expense' plus 'Other operating expenses'. The explicit D&A rows appear as separate operating lines above EBIT, suggesting D&A is outside those selected rows, but the excerpt does not show any SG&A-specific, overhead-specific, or R&D-specific D&A row. Because the available D&A lines are unallocated totals and the main summary includes impairment, assigning them to XSGA would be speculative. Conservative output is no separate XSGA_DA selection pending manual review.
